... [320 lines omitted] ...
94→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 124→def test_missing_required_field_errors() -> None: 125→ """Missing required field produces error.""" 134→ assert data["error_type"] == "invalid_request" 137→def test_invalid_json_errors() -> None: 138→ """Invalid JSON input produces error.""" 149→ assert data["error_type"] == "invalid_json" 152→def test_non_object_json_errors() -> None: 153→ """Non-object JSON input produces error.""" 164→ assert data["error_type"] == "invalid_json" 167→# -- Error handling tests -- 170→def test_agent_cli_error_serialized() -> None: 171→ """AgentCliError is serialized as JSON error envelope.""" 176→ name="error_tool", 177→ description="Errors out", 179→ @click.command("error-cmd") 180→ def error_cmd(*, request: SimpleRequest) -> None: 181→ raise AgentCliError("something went wrong", error_type="cli_error") 185→ result = runner.invoke(error_cmd, []) 190→ assert data["error_type"] == "cli_error" 251→ assert "error_schema" in doc 252→ assert doc["error_schema"]["properties"]["success"]["const"] is False

1→--- 2→title: "@json_command Decorator" 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding @json_command decorator to a CLI command" 5→ - "creating structured JSON CLI output" 6→ - "understanding emit_json patterns" 7→ - "implementing output_types validation" 8→ - "working with AgentCliError error handling" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "applying @json_command below @click.command in the decorator stack" 11→ warning: "@json_command must be applied ABOVE @click.command. The correct order is @mcp_exposed > @json_command > @click.command." 12→ - action: "using json.dumps without indent=2 in a @json_command context" 13→ warning: "All json.dumps() calls in @json_command commands use indent=2 for pretty-printing." 14→ - action: "raising an exception in a @json_command without using AgentCliError" 15→ warning: "Use AgentCliError(message, error_type=...) to ensure errors serialize as JSON when --json is active." 16→ - action: "declaring output_types that don't match the return annotation" 17→ warning: "test_output_types_matches_return_annotation() validates output_types against return type hints. Mismatches fail CI." 18→--- 19→ 20→# @json_command Decorator 21→ 22→Universal CLI JSON infrastructure for agent-optimized command output. 23→ 24→**Source**: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/json_command.py` 25→ 26→## Decorator Parameters 27→ 28→```python 29→@json_command( 30→ exclude_json_input=frozenset({"repo_id"}),
... [89 lines omitted] ...
67→3. Raises `TypeError` if neither is available 69→## Error Handling 71→`AgentCliError` extends `click.ClickException` with an `error_type` parameter. See `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/errors.py` for the current class definition. 73→Import: `from erk_shared.agentclick.errors import AgentCliError` 75→When `--json` is active, `AgentCliError` serializes as: 78→{ "success": false, "error_type": "invalid_plan", "message": "Plan not found" } 81→Three error categories: 83→- **Validation errors**: `error_type: "invalid_json_input"` (bad stdin JSON) 84→- **Command errors**: Custom `error_type` from `AgentCliError` 85→- **Passthrough**: `SystemExit` and non-Click exceptions pass through unchanged 102→- Mismatches fail the test with descriptive error messages

1→--- 2→title: "Adding --json to CLI Commands" 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding --json flag to a CLI command" 5→ - "exposing a command via MCP" 6→ - "creating result dataclasses for JSON output" 7→ - "stacking @mcp_exposed and @json_command decorators" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "placing @mcp_exposed below @json_command in decorator stack" 10→ warning: "@mcp_exposed must be ABOVE @json_command. Correct order: @mcp_exposed > @json_command > @click.command." 11→ - action: "creating a result dataclass without to_json_dict() method" 12→ warning: "Result dataclasses should implement to_json_dict() for custom serialization. Without it, emit_json_result() falls back to dataclasses.asdict() which may not handle complex types correctly." 13→--- 14→ 15→# Adding --json to CLI Commands 16→ 17→Step-by-step guide to adding structured JSON output to existing Click commands. 18→ 19→## Decorator Stacking Order 20→ 21→The decorators must be stacked in this exact order (outermost to innermost): 22→ 23→1. `@mcp_exposed(...)` — MCP exposure (optional, outermost) 24→2. `@json_command(...)` — JSON infrastructure 25→3. `@click.command(...)` — Click command 26→4. `@click.option(...)` — Options/arguments 27→5. `@click.pass_obj` — Context passing (innermost) 28→ 29→See `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py` for a working example of this stack. 30→
... [82 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Erk MCP Integration 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding new MCP tools to erk" 5→ - "configuring erk as an MCP server for Claude" 6→ - "understanding the erk-mcp package structure" 7→ - "debugging MCP tool calls from external clients" 8→--- 9→ 10→# Erk MCP Integration 11→ 12→Erk exposes its capabilities as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server via the `erk-mcp` package. This allows external MCP clients (e.g., Claude Desktop, other AI tools) to invoke erk operations. 13→ 14→## Package Structure 15→ 16→<!-- Source: packages/erk-mcp/src/erk_mcp/server.py --> 17→ 18→``` 19→packages/erk-mcp/ 20→├── pyproject.toml # Package definition, depends on fastmcp>=2.0 21→└── src/erk_mcp/ 22→ ├── __init__.py 23→ ├── __main__.py # Entry point: parses --transport/--host/--port, calls create_mcp().run() 24→ └── server.py # Tool definitions and _run_erk() wrapper 25→``` 26→ 27→The package depends on [FastMCP](https://github.com/jlowin/fastmcp) (`fastmcp>=2.0`) and is a thin wrapper that delegates to the `erk` CLI. 28→ 29→## CLI Wrappers 30→
... [105 lines omitted] ...
35→- **Standard wrapper** — Runs `erk <args>`, raises `RuntimeError` on non-zero exit. Used by hand-written tools (`plan_list`, `plan_view`). 36→- **JSON wrapper** — Runs `erk <command> --json`, pipes params as JSON stdin. Does NOT raise on non-zero exit — `--json` guarantees structured error output flows through to the agent. Used by auto-discovered MCP tools. 62→Dispatches a task for fully autonomous execution via `erk one-shot --json`. Returns structured JSON indicating success or failure. On success, includes the created PR reference and CI run details. With `dry_run`: preview without executing.

session.assistant: Now update the tripwires and index references:
session.tool_result: 1→--- 2→title: Cli Tripwires 3→read_when: 4→ - "working on cli code" 5→--- 6→ 7→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 8→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 9→<!-- Generated from cli/*.md frontmatter --> 10→ 11→# Cli Tripwires 12→ 13→Rules triggered by matching actions in code. 14→ 15→**Design batch commands that process items despite validation failures** → Read [Batch Exec Commands](batch-exec-commands.md) first. Validate ALL items upfront before processing ANY items. Stop on first validation error. 16→ 17→**Return non-zero exit codes for batch command failures** → Read [Batch Exec Commands](batch-exec-commands.md) first. Always exit 0, encode errors in JSON output with per-item success fields. 18→ 19→**Use OR semantics for batch success (success=true if any item succeeds)** → Read [Batch Exec Commands](batch-exec-commands.md) first. Use AND semantics: top-level success=true only if ALL items succeed. 20→ 21→**Using RuntimeError for expected CLI failures** → Read [CLI Error Handling Anti-Patterns](error-handling-antipatterns.md) first. RuntimeError signals a programmer error (bug in the code), NOT expected user-facing failures. Use UserFacingCliError for expected conditions (missing files, invalid input, precondition violations) that should exit cleanly with an actionable message. 22→ 23→**WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP maps command names to .yml filenames** → Read [Workflow Commands](workflow-commands.md) first. command names intentionally diverge from filenames (e.g., pr-rebase → pr-rebase.yml, but plan-implement → plan-implement.yml via DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME constant) 24→ 25→**adding --from-current-branch without handling detached HEAD** → Read [Slot Assign --from-current-branch](slot-assign-from-current-branch.md) first. When the target branch is checked out in another worktree, fall back to detached HEAD checkout. See slot-assign-from-current-branch.md. 26→ 27→**adding --json flag to a command without using @json_output decorator** → Read [Agent-Friendly CLI Design Principles](agent-friendly-cli.md) first. Use the shared @json_output decorator from src/erk/cli/json_output.py. Do not manually implement JSON serialization in individual commands. 28→ 29→**adding --sync to checkout** → Read [Checkout/Teleport Command Split](checkout-teleport-split.md) first. checkout is local-only; use teleport for sync. Checkout preserves local state; teleport force-resets to remote. 30→
session.assistant: The tripwires file is auto-generated from frontmatter, so it'll be regenerated. Let me also check the cli index:

5→ 6→- **[activation-scripts.md](activation-scripts.md)** — working with worktree environment setup, understanding .erk/activate.sh scripts, configuring post-create commands 7→- **[adding-json-to-commands.md](adding-json-to-commands.md)** — adding --json flag to a CLI command, exposing a command via MCP, creating result dataclasses for JSON output, stacking @mcp_exposed and @json_command decorators 8→- **[agent-friendly-cli.md](agent-friendly-cli.md)** — adding --json flag to a CLI command, creating or modifying MCP tools, implementing structured JSON output for CLI commands, working on agent-friendly CLI patterns, implementing erk schema command 9→- **[ambiguity-resolution.md](ambiguity-resolution.md)** — implementing CLI commands that accept identifiers with multiple possible matches, designing CLI behavior for ambiguous input, displaying tables of options without interactive selection 10→- **[auto-generated-reference-docs.md](auto-generated-reference-docs.md)** — adding or modifying CLI commands, changing erk exec command structure, CI fails with exec reference check error 11→- **[backend-aware-display.md](backend-aware-display.md)** — adding CLI commands that behave differently for issue vs planned-PR plans, routing between gh issue and gh pr commands based on plan backend, understanding how plan_backend affects CLI output 12→- **[batch-exec-commands.md](batch-exec-commands.md)** — implementing batch operations for exec commands, designing JSON stdin/stdout interfaces for erk exec, understanding batch command success semantics 13→- **[checkout-helpers.md](checkout-helpers.md)** — writing checkout commands, creating worktrees in checkout commands, implementing branch checkout logic 14→- **[checkout-teleport-split.md](checkout-teleport-split.md)** — working with erk pr checkout or erk pr teleport commands, understanding the difference between checkout and teleport, modifying cmux-open-pr command
